title: BasicEma cannot produce a meaningful 10-period EMA from its sample data

evidence.quote: if index < indicator.windowSize()-1 { return &decimal.ZERO } ... if index == indicator.windowSize()-1 { value := firstValueFallback(index)

reasoning: The example builds a one-candle time series but configures a 10-period EMA. The only in-range candle index is 0, and EMA deliberately returns ZERO for indexes below window-1. The first seed index for a 10-period EMA is 9, but calculating it asks the close-price indicator for Candles[9], which is out of range for the one-candle sample. A user following the example gets either a zero value or a panic instead of a usable EMA result.
reproduction: ema := example.BasicEma(); ema.Calculate(0) returns decimal.ZERO; ema.Calculate(9) panics on the underlying one-candle series.
recommendation: Seed at least 10 candles for the 10-period EMA, lower the EMA window to match the sample length, or make the example accept a caller-provided populated time series.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests exercise example.BasicEma or verify that the example can calculate a non-zero EMA value at a valid index.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an example package test that calls BasicEma(), calculates the first expected non-zero EMA index, and asserts it does not panic and returns the expected value.
minimumFixScope: Update example/basic.go sample data and, if needed, the StrategyExample assumptions that depend on BasicEma.
